items.title: Enzo Fernandez trolls England fans with ‘Wonderwall’ post on Instagram after Argentina KO Three Lions’ World Cup dream
items.summary: ENZO FERNANDEZ trolled England after knocking them out of the World Cup with a brutal Wonderwall post. The Chelsea midfielder uploaded a series of images to his Instagram story on Thursday, just hours after the dramatic semi-final comeback. Fernandez, 25, sparked the sensational turnaround in Atlant

items.title: England vs Argentina: A one-sided rugby rivalry - will Pumas bite back?
items.summary: Fresh from the drama of Wednesday's FIFA World Cup semi-final between England and Argentina, the nations meet again on Saturday as part of rugby's Nations Championship in Santiago del Estero.

items.title: MVG under pressure? Nijman can dethrone Littler? World Matchplay talking points
items.summary: Michael van Gerwen can erase concerns about his erratic form at the World Matchplay, while Wessel Nijman has emerged as a serious title threat in Blackpool.

items.title: Effort, spirit and moments - but do England lack all-round quality?
items.summary: As England reflect on their World Cup semi-final defeat by Argentina, BBC Sport's Phil McNulty looks at what Thomas Tuchel's team were lacking.

items.title: Who am I? Guess World Cup star No 40
items.summary: Will you get today's player? Try to guess his identity in as few attempts as possible.

items.title: Fox’s US World Cup summer: wild mispronunciations, Corden’s sad beers and Lowe’s excellence
items.summary: The broadcaster’s tournament coverage was a mix of flat and fizz. It could also have been a long farewell with World Cup rights up for grabs Goodbye, then, to Fox, to its band of upbeat Brits and grown men dressed in suits and sneakers. Goodbye to constant cutaways to Gianni Infantino in the stands
